sections.paragraph:
f. (with Kametz impure), from the root חָזָה-

(1) appearance, aspect, especially of something grand or handsome, (compare מַרְאֶה). Dan 8:5, קֶרֶן חָזוּת a conspicuous or great horn, verse , חָזוּת אַרְבַּע וַתַּעֲלֶינָה “and there arose four conspicuous (horns).” For it appears that it must be thus interpreted on account of verse .

(2) a prophetic vision, Isa 21:2.

(3) a revelation, a law, hence a covenant (both ideas being kindred to the minds of the Hebrews, with whom religion was a covenant with God). Isa 28:18 (compare חֹזֶה verse 15 Isa 28:15); 29:11 .
